name: Central African Republic

visa_free.notes: Visa-free under CEMAC free movement agreement (effective October 31, 2017); national ID card acceptable in lieu of passport
Visa-free under CEMAC free movement agreement (effective October 31, 2017); national ID card acceptable in lieu of passport
Visa-free under CEMAC free movement agreement (effective October 31, 2017); national ID card acceptable in lieu of passport
Visa-free under CEMAC free movement agreement (effective October 31, 2017); national ID card acceptable in lieu of passport
Visa-free under CEMAC free movement agreement (effective October 31, 2017); national ID card acceptable in lieu of passport
Visa-free entry up to 90 days; confirmed by Wikipedia canonical list

Visa-free entry up to 90 days per Wikipedia's canonical 14-country list. Note: NOT listed in the evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ's 17-country exempt list - discrepancy between sources. Retained on the stronger Wikipedia aggregation which tracks CAR MFA policy announcements.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival established October 2019 per UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs; must be pre-arranged at a CAR diplomatic mission abroad before travel - confirmed by evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ ('A visa on arrival can be obtained only if it was pre-arranged at a CAR diplomatic mission abroad'). Max stay duration not specified on any accessible official source.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not in visa-free list
e_visa.notes: Tourist e-visa available via evisa-centrafrique.com (operated by DigitalGate SARL, a third-party referencing the CAR Ministry of Foreign Affairs). Only tourist purpose (individual and group). Cannot be extended inside or outside CAR. Visas from other African nations are NOT accepted for CAR entry. Visa on arrival is only available if pre-arranged at a CAR diplomatic mission abroad. Entry via e-visa is only permitted at Bangui city airport. Stay duration: 1 to 180 days per FAQ. Note: on 2026-06-29 re-fetch the portal no longer shows an explicit 'testing mode' notice - status of operational readiness unconfirmed from any official .gouv.cf source.

visa_policy.notes: The official CAR MFA domain (diplomatie.gouv.cf) uses a self-signed SSL certificate preventing content extraction on all verification runs. The CAR Embassy in Washington (usrcaembassy.org) page returned empty content on 2026-06-29 re-verification. Official/diplomatic passport holders: no exemption exists per prior verification ('No change. Visa applications will be processed by the Embassy'). Effective February 12, 2024, embassy/international organization staff (UN, EU, NGOs, embassy contractors) must obtain prior MFA authorization before entering CAR. The US visa exemption was cancelled effective November 17, 2023 (confirmed by Wikipedia tracking the CAR MFA announcement). The canonical visa-free list is 14 nationalities per Wikipedia (live-fetched 2026-06-29 - unchanged). The evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ (non-official portal referencing CAR MFA) lists 17 exemptions - the 3 additional entries (DRC, Nigeria, and USA) are not confirmed by any official government source and have been removed or kept out of visa_free per safety rules (a false visa-free is the worst error). DRC and Nigeria moved to gaps. USA confirmed revoked. The portal is operated by DigitalGate SARL; its explicit 'testing mode' notice was absent on 2026-06-29 re-fetch but operational status of e-visa processing is unconfirmed from any official source.

visa_types.name: Visa Court Séjour - Tourisme (Short-Stay Tourist Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term entry for tourism and leisure purposes. Applied at a CAR embassy or consulate abroad.
visa_types.notes: Fee schedule from CAR Embassy Washington: 0–1 month $150, express processing +$50. Application requires passport, 2 ID photos, and supporting documents. Postal return requires stamped envelope with CHRONOPOST insurance. E-visa option (evisa-centrafrique.com) exists but operational status unconfirmed.

visa_types.name: Visa Court Séjour 45 Jours (Short-Stay 45-Day Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term entry visa valid for up to 45 days, for tourism or short business visits.
visa_types.notes: Fee schedule from CAR Embassy Washington: 45 days $175, express processing +$50. Applied at CAR embassies/consulates abroad.

visa_types.name: Visa Court Séjour - Affaires (Short-Stay Business Visa, 2–3 Months)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for business purposes valid for two to three months.
visa_types.notes: Fee schedule from CAR Embassy Washington: 2–3 months $250, express processing +$50. Applied at CAR embassies/consulates abroad.

visa_types.name: Visa Court Séjour - Long Duration (Short-Stay Visa 3+ Months)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for stays exceeding three months, for purposes such as extended visits, research, or short-term professional assignments.
visa_types.notes: Fee schedule from CAR Embassy Washington: 3+ months $300, express processing +$50. Exact maximum stay not specified in official sources; long-stay visa (visa long séjour) is the formal category for extended residence.

visa_types.name: Visa Long Séjour (Long-Stay Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term entry visa for work, study, family reunification, or extended residence in the Central African Republic. Issued by CAR embassies and consulates abroad.
visa_types.notes: Official category listed by the CAR Embassy in Paris. Application accepts online form (with account creation) or paper PDF form. Payment by bank transfer or cash at embassy. Specific fee, validity, and stay duration not published on accessible official pages. Covers work, study, and residence purposes.

visa_types.name: E-Visa (Visa Électronique Touristique)
visa_types.purpose: Electronic tourist visa for individual or group tourism, available via the CAR Ministry of Foreign Affairs-referenced e-visa portal. Entry only permitted at Bangui M'Poko International Airport.
visa_types.notes: Operated by DigitalGate SARL (third-party referencing CAR MFA); portal's explicit 'testing mode' notice was absent on 2026-06-29 re-fetch but e-visa processing operational status is unconfirmed from any official .gouv.cf source. Stay duration 1–180 days. Cannot be extended inside or outside CAR. Entry only at Bangui city airport. Visa-exempt nationalities and VoA holders are excluded from this requirement. Not a .gouv.cf official government domain.

visa_types.name: Visa Diplomatique / Officiel (Diplomatic/Official Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for holders of diplomatic or official passports travelling to the Central African Republic on official business. Despite diplomatic passport status, a visa is still required.
visa_types.notes: CAR Embassy Washington confirmed in prior verification: 'No change. Visa applications will be processed by the Embassy' for diplomatic/official passport holders - no exemption exists. Effective February 12, 2024, embassy staff, UN, EU, NGO personnel, and international organization contractors must obtain prior MFA authorization before entering CAR.

cbi.notes: No active official Citizenship-by-Investment program exists as of 2026. The government proposed a crypto-based CBI program via the Sango Project (USD 60,000 in Sango Coins locked for 5 years for citizenship; USD 6,000 locked for 3 years for e-residency). The Constitutional Court ruled this unconstitutional, holding that 'nationality has no market value' and residency can only be conferred on those physically present in the country. The Sango Coin program is not active.

agreements: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) free movement of persons - member states Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Republic of Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon; agreement signed 2013, ratified and effective October 31, 2017
ECCAS (Economic Community of Central African States) - Protocol on Freedom of Movement adopted 1983; broader regional framework with limited practical implementation beyond CEMAC sub-bloc

validation_notes: 2026-06-29: RE-VERIFIED - Wikipedia live-fetched 2026-06-29 confirms the same 14 visa-free nationalities as the current record: Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cameroon, Chad, Republic of the Congo, Côte d'Ivoire, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Israel, Liberia, Niger, Rwanda, Senegal. No changes to visa_free list.
2026-06-29: RE-VERIFIED - Wikipedia confirms UAE visa on arrival (since October 2019) with pre-arrangement requirement unchanged.
2026-06-29: RE-VERIFIED - Wikipedia confirms US visa exemption cancelled November 17, 2023. USA correctly excluded from visa_free.

2026-06-29: NOTE - evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ re-fetched 2026-06-29. The explicit 'testing mode' notice previously reported is no longer visible in fetched content; portal presents e-visa system as functional. However, this is a non-official third-party portal (DigitalGate SARL), and no .gouv.cf official source confirms e-visa operational status. E-visa notes updated to reflect this uncertainty. No change to e_visa classification.
2026-06-29: CONFIRMED - diplomatie.gouv.cf still returns SSL error (self-signed certificate); content not extractable.
2026-06-29: CONFIRMED - usrcaembassy.org/visa still returns empty/header-only content.
2026-06-29: CONFIRMED - cf.usembassy.gov alert pages still return Technical Difficulties.
2026-06-29: NO CHANGES made to visa_free, visa_on_arrival, e_visa, or conditional_access lists - all existing data re-confirmed or preserved per safety rules.

2026-06-27: REMOVED DRC (COD) from visa_free - previously added based on evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ (non-official portal by DigitalGate SARL). On 2026-06-27 re-verification, Wikipedia's canonical 14-country list (live-fetched) does NOT include DRC. No official .gouv.cf source confirms this. Per safety rule (false visa-free = worst error), DRC removed and moved to gaps pending official confirmation.

2026-06-27: REMOVED Nigeria (NGA) from visa_free - previously added based on evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ (non-official). Wikipedia's canonical 14-country list does NOT include Nigeria. No official .gouv.cf source confirms this. Removed per safety rules; added to gaps.

2026-06-27: CONFIRMED USA remains excluded from visa_free - Wikipedia (live-fetched) explicitly records the US exemption was cancelled November 17, 2023. The evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ still lists USA as exempt but this is an outdated non-official portal entry. USA correctly remains excluded.
2026-06-27: CONFIRMED visa_free list of 14 nationalities aligns with Wikipedia's canonical list.

2026-06-27: RETAINED Niger (NER) in visa_free - Wikipedia confirms Niger in the 14-country canonical list. The evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ does not list Niger among its 17, creating a discrepancy. Wikipedia's tracking of CAR MFA policy is the stronger signal; Niger retained with discrepancy noted.
2026-06-27: CONFIRMED UAE visa_on_arrival status - evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ confirms pre-arrangement requirement; no change.
CONFIRMED cbi has_program=false: Sango Project ruled unconstitutional by CAR Constitutional Court; no active program
CONFIRMED conditional_access remains empty: no credential-based or special-status exemptions found from any official source
gaps: diplomatie.gouv.cf (official CAR MFA) uses a self-signed SSL certificate - content not extractable on 2026-06-29 re-verification; no official enumeration of visa-exempt nationalities obtained from this source

usrcaembassy.org/visa (CAR Embassy Washington) returned empty/header-only content on 2026-06-29 re-fetch - fee schedule and visa policies from prior run (0-1 month $150, 45-day $175, 2-3 month $250, 3+ month $300) retained but could not be re-confirmed live

cf.usembassy.gov alert pages returned Technical Difficulties on 2026-06-29 re-fetch - US Embassy Bangui alerts about November 2023 US exemption cancellation and February 2024 policy changes not directly re-confirmed from that source (confirmed instead via Wikipedia)

DRC (Democratic Republic of the Congo, COD): listed as visa-exempt by evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ but NOT by Wikipedia's canonical 14-country list. No official .gouv.cf source available. Status unconfirmed; classified as visa_required per safety rules pending official confirmation.

Nigeria (NGA): listed as visa-exempt by evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ but NOT by Wikipedia's canonical 14-country list. No official .gouv.cf source available. Status unconfirmed; classified as visa_required per safety rules pending official confirmation.

Niger (NER): listed as visa-free by Wikipedia's 14-country canonical list but NOT present in evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ's 17-country list. Retained in visa_free based on Wikipedia, but discrepancy unresolved.

USA: evisa-centrafrique.com FAQ still lists USA as exempt despite the November 17, 2023 cancellation confirmed by Wikipedia and prior US Embassy alerts. The evisa portal appears outdated on this point.

E-visa portal (evisa-centrafrique.com): explicit 'testing mode' notice absent on 2026-06-29 re-fetch; portal presents e-visa as functional but this is a non-official third-party site. No official .gouv.cf source confirms whether e-visa processing is now operationally available.
max_stay_days for UAE visa on arrival: not specified on any accessible official source.
No official CAR government page listing visa fees verified from .gouv.cf domain on 2026-06-29 re-run.
No official government source found for RBI, digital nomad, fast-track, or talent programs.
No conditional_access rules (credential-based, special-status, or diplomatic-passport-type waivers) confirmed from any official CAR source.

outbound_official.note: No official Central African Republic government outbound visa-list page located for ordinary passport holders. The CAR Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.cf) is live but SSL error prevents content extraction. No comprehensive, publicly published visa-free/VOA/e-visa destination list for ordinary CAR passport holders exists on any .gouv.cf page found during verification runs.
